title: Reed's Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
context_text: Reed conjectured in 1998 that the chromatic number is at most the average, up to rounding, of the trivial upper bound $\Delta(G)+1$ and the clique lower bound $\omega(G)$. King and Reed had previously shown that the $\varepsilon$-version holds for $\varepsilon \leq 1/130{,}000$ when $\Delta$ is large enough.
paper_contribution: Proves that $\chi(G) \leq \lceil \frac{25}{26}(\Delta+1) + \frac{1}{26}\omega \rceil$ holds for every graph $G$ with sufficiently large maximum degree (Theorem 1.7), advancing the best known $\varepsilon$ in the $\varepsilon$-version from $1/130{,}000$ to $1/26$.
notes: PDF source; statement is clearly readable.
paper_title: Colouring Graphs with Sparse Neighbourhoods: Bounds and Applications

title: Question 1.3
statement_text: Let $G$ be a $\lfloor (1-\varepsilon)(\Delta(G)+1) \rfloor + 1$-critical graph with $\omega(G) \leq (1-\alpha)(\Delta(G)+1)$. What is the largest $\delta = \delta(\varepsilon,\alpha)$, such that $G$ is $\delta$-sparse?
context_text: This question isolates the first of two steps in the King–Reed proof strategy for Reed's conjecture: showing that a graph which is critical with respect to a list assignment and has bounded clique number must have sparse neighbourhoods. Improving $\delta$ directly strengthens the $\varepsilon$ achievable in Theorem 1.2.
notes: PDF source; statement is clearly readable.
paper_title: Colouring Graphs with Sparse Neighbourhoods: Bounds and Applications

title: Question 1.4
statement_text: Let $G$ be a $\delta$-sparse graph. What is the largest $\varepsilon = \varepsilon(\delta)$ such that $\chi(G) \leq (1-\varepsilon)(\Delta+1)$?
context_text: This isolates the second step in the King–Reed proof: given neighbourhood sparsity, bound the chromatic number below $\Delta+1$. Molloy–Reed obtained $\varepsilon(\delta)\approx 0.0238\delta$ and Bruhn–Joos improved to $\approx 0.1827\delta - 0.0778\delta^{3/2}$; the paper further improves this using an iterative naive colouring procedure.
notes: PDF source; statement is clearly readable.
paper_title: Colouring Graphs with Sparse Neighbourhoods: Bounds and Applications

title: Erdős–Nešetřil Conjecture (Conjecture 1.8)
context_text: Erdős and Nešetřil conjectured this tight upper bound on the strong chromatic index; tightness is witnessed by blow-ups of the 5-cycle. Molloy–Reed gave the first non-trivial upper bound, and Bruhn–Joos subsequently proved $\chi'_s(G) \leq 1.93\,\Delta^2$ for sufficiently large $\Delta$.
paper_contribution: Proves $\chi'_s(G) \leq 1.835\,\Delta(G)^2$ for sufficiently large $\Delta(G)$ (Theorem 1.11) by combining improved neighbourhood-sparsity bounds for a critical subgraph of $L^2(G)$ with the iterative naive colouring procedure of Theorem 1.6.
notes: PDF source; year of original conjecture not stated explicitly in the paper.
paper_title: Colouring Graphs with Sparse Neighbourhoods: Bounds and Applications
